frameworks.name: difference-between-best-and-next-best posterior
frameworks.one_line: confidence reflects gap between top two posterior probabilities

frameworks.name: Bayesian confidence hypothesis
frameworks.one_line: confidence equals posterior probability of chosen option

frameworks.name: posterior entropy account
frameworks.one_line: confidence reflects overall uncertainty across alternatives

frameworks.name: signal-detection / multi-alternative decision theory
frameworks.one_line: extends two-alternative confidence theory to >2 options

core_question: In multi-alternative decisions, what computational quantity does confidence track?
key_assumptions: observers compute posterior probabilities over alternatives
two-alternative confidence theories reduce to a special case
confidence is a graded report tied to decision quality
manipulations: number of alternatives expanded to three
visual categorization task with varying posterior probability profiles
key_findings: confidence best explained by difference between best and next-best posterior
posterior probability of chosen option alone fits worse
interpretations: confidence indexes subjective probability of having made the best possible decision
leading Bayesian confidence hypothesis is overturned in multi-alternative settings
